title: Secrets policy, .env.example, secret-scanning CI check
objective: Document every planned secret (name, owning service, storage location, rotation, never-in-git rule) in docs/SECRETS_POLICY.md; add .env.example files (names only, no values) for services/api and apps/web; add a deterministic repo-local secret-scan (tools-free Python script + new standalone GitHub Actions workflow) that fails CI on likely credentials in the tree or new commits. No external paid actions; zero secrets required to run.
business_reason: Prevents credential leakage before any cloud key arrives; makes the no-secrets-in-git rule mechanically enforced (PRD 17/25)
inputs: project-control/reports/M0-T004-G5-security-review.md (defect list: SHA-pinning required before secrets land; known secret patterns rnd_/sbp_/eyJ/service_role)
outputs: .github/workflows/secret-scan.yml (new standalone workflow, contents: read, actions SHA-pinned per G5 Defect 1)

acceptance_scenarios.expected: secret_scan.py exits 0; secret-scan workflow green on a push with no credentials

acceptance_scenarios.input: scratch file containing realistic fake credentials (Render rnd_..., Supabase sbp_..., JWT eyJ..., service_role, PEM private key, AWS AKIA..., generic api_key=)
acceptance_scenarios.expected: secret_scan.py exits nonzero listing file, line, and pattern class for each; no credential value echoed in full (mask middle)

acceptance_scenarios.input: legitimate lookalikes (sha512 integrity hashes in package-lock.json, .env.example placeholder names, docs mentioning pattern names)
acceptance_scenarios.expected: no false positive: allowlist mechanism (path or inline pragma) documented and used; package-lock.json integrity hashes pass
every secret named in docs/SECRETS_POLICY.md appears in the correct .env.example with a comment and NO value; .gitignore already blocks real .env files (verify, do not edit .gitignore)
permissions contents: read only; actions pinned to full commit SHAs with version comments (G5 Defect 1 standard); no secrets referenced; runs on push and pull_request
existing ci.yml untouched; existing jobs unaffected; scan runtime under 1 minute on this repo

risks: Regex-based scanning has inherent false-negative risk; policy doc must state it complements (not replaces) GitHub push protection, which the owner should enable in repo settings (human action)

progress_log.message: G0 contracting: scope set to new standalone files only (SECRETS_POLICY.md, .env.example x2, secret_scan.py, secret-scan.yml) to remain disjoint from M0-T006 rework (ci.yml) and M0-T009 (validate_contracts.py). Repo-local stdlib scanner chosen over external actions (no license/secret dependencies).
